# UC-14.1.20 · Air Quality and CO2 Monitoring (Meraki MT)

## Description

Monitors indoor air quality to ensure safe working conditions.

## Value

Monitors indoor air quality to ensure safe working conditions.

## Implementation

Monitor CO2 and air quality sensor data. Alert on high levels.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=meraki:api sensor_type="air_quality"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor CO2 and air quality sensor data. Alert on high levels.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" sensor_type="air_quality" co2_ppm=*
| stats latest(co2_ppm) as current_co2, avg(co2_ppm) as avg_co2 by location
| where current_co2 > 1000
```

Understanding this SPL

**Air Quality and CO2 Monitoring (Meraki MT)** — Monitors indoor air quality to ensure safe working conditions.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=meraki:api sensor_type="air_quality"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_network; **sourcetype**: meraki:api. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_network, sourcetype="meraki:api". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by location** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where current_co2 > 1000` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search in a known window. Spot-check a few events against the BMS console (for example Niagara, Tridium, or Honeywell) and confirm zones, values, and alarms line up. Verify field names and that the right roles can search the index.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: CO2 level gauge per location; trend timeline; air quality status chart.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" sensor_type="air_quality" co2_ppm=*
| stats latest(co2_ppm) as current_co2, avg(co2_ppm) as avg_co2 by location
| where current_co2 > 1000
```

## Visualization

CO2 level gauge per location; trend timeline; air quality status chart.
